text:
(1) No license to operate motor vehicles in this state shall be issued to an habitual offender, nor shall an habitual offender operate a motor vehicle in this state:

(a) For a period of five years from the date of the order of the department finding such person to be an habitual offender except as may be permitted by section 42-2-132.5 ; and

(b) Until such time as financial responsibility requirements are met.
markdown:
## Title 42
#### Article 2. Drivers’ Licenses
##### § 42-2-205 — Prohibition

(1) No license to operate motor vehicles in this state shall be issued to an habitual offender, nor shall an habitual offender operate a motor vehicle in this state:

    (a) For a period of five years from the date of the order of the department finding such person to be an habitual offender except as may be permitted by section 42-2-132.5 ; and

    (b) Until such time as financial responsibility requirements are met.

subsections.text: No license to operate motor vehicles in this state shall be issued to an habitual offender, nor shall an habitual offender operate a motor vehicle in this state:

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: For a period of five years from the date of the order of the department finding such person to be an habitual offender except as may be permitted by section 42-2-132.5 ; and

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Until such time as financial responsibility requirements are met.
